Content: Stamos: Just even taking like what's the new schedule out of it for a second, it's like can we finish the game for, you know, the remaining amount of money?
Content_zh: Stamos：先不談新時程這件事，問題是我們能不能用剩下的錢把遊戲做完？

Content: Because I think we're in a situation where we need to significantly increase our funding for this game.
Content_zh: 因為我覺得我們現在的狀況是，必須大幅增加這款遊戲的資金。

Content: Or significantly reduce the scope of the game.
Content_zh: 不然就得大幅縮小遊戲規模。

Content: [Music] Rice: OK.
Content_zh: [音樂] Rice：好。

Content: Stamos: That said, really talking about the overall schedule.
Content_zh: Stamos：也就是說，我們其實是在談整體時程。

Content: Right now the overall schedule for Red's is posted as April.
Content_zh: 目前 Red’s 的整體時程是排在四月。

Content: And I think there's a sense from the team that that's not actually a feasible timeframe.
Content_zh: 但我覺得團隊其實覺得，那個時間不太可行。

Content: Min: I think, I think just the larger goal is when, like hey, when's our Alpha?
Content_zh: Min：我想，更大的目標就是，嘿，我們的 Alpha 是什麼時候？

Content: What's that mean?
Content_zh: 那代表什麼？

Content: When's our Beta?
Content_zh: Beta 是什麼時候？

Content: When do we ship?
Content_zh: 我們什麼時候推出？

Content: When's that, our big trailer coming.
Content_zh: 那支大預告什麼時候出來？

Content: Those are sort of like, like the few hi level stuff that I like to kind of get an idea of.
Content_zh: 這些算是我想先掌握的幾個大方向。

Content: When are the ports coming online?
Content_zh: 各平台移植什麼時候會上線？

Content: So it does worry me, because that does make a big difference in terms of what to do now.
Content_zh: 所以這確實讓我擔心，因為那會大大影響現在該做什麼。

Content: Crook: And for me, like dates and things, just working here for this long...
Content_zh: Crook：對我來說，日期之類的事，在這裡工作這麼久……

Content: I've just gotten used to things change and so I don't freak out.
Content_zh: 我已經習慣事情會變，所以不會抓狂。

Content: And I think, you know, there were times where it was, yeah, we've had people freak out and like leave because they freaked out.
Content_zh: 我想，以前確實有過大家抓狂，甚至有人抓狂到離開的時候。

Content: And so I kind of learned that, you just sort of, we'll figure it out.
Content_zh: 所以我學到的是，你就是先做下去，我們總會想出辦法。

Content: Stamos: Not enough of work has been done for the team to have confidence that they're really halfway through.
Content_zh: Stamos：目前完成的工作還不夠，團隊沒辦法有信心說真的已經做到一半。

Content: Stamos: And I think there's a lot of work to do still, to understand really what the scope is,
Content_zh: Stamos：我覺得還有很多工作要做，才能真正理解範圍有多大，

Content: but it's coming into, it's coming into view.
Content_zh: 但它正在慢慢變清楚。

Content: I think it's expanded a lot from what they originally thought.
Content_zh: 我覺得它比他們一開始想的擴大很多。

Content: And when projects evolve like that, you need to really reevaluate the plans.
Content_zh: 專案發展成那樣時，就真的需要重新評估計畫。

Content: So having a clear plan in place that you can iterate on is a good start.
Content_zh: 所以先有一個清楚、可以反覆調整的計畫，是好的開始。

Content: So I want to get like the tools in place and help Greg get the tools in place to have that, so that there's a plan that he can easily iterate on.
Content_zh: 所以我想把工具準備好，也幫 Greg 把工具準備好，讓他有一份能輕鬆反覆調整的計畫。

Content: So that's mostly what I'm helping with.
Content_zh: 這大概就是我主要在幫忙的事。

Content: Stamos: Job number one is to make this game awesome, because it's just too important to do anything else.
Content_zh: Stamos：第一要務就是把這款遊戲做得很棒，因為它太重要了，其他事都得讓路。

Content: First steps first is to figure out at least where we're landing.
Content_zh: 第一步至少要先弄清楚我們大概會落在哪裡。

Content: And so that's not pick a date and work back from that.
Content_zh: 所以不是先挑一個日期，再往回推。

Content: It is more of a bottoms-up.
Content_zh: 比較像是由下而上估算。

Content: Let's map out everything that we know about the game and then really see how long it will take.
Content_zh: 我們把已知的遊戲內容全部列出來，再真正看需要多久。

Content: Schafer: Like can we do that before this milestone is over or after?
Content_zh: Schafer：像是我們能在這個里程碑結束前做到嗎？還是要之後？

Content: 'Cause what we'll find out in this milestone is if we can bring, if we can bring, Cloud Colony to Alpha to Beta that'll mean something.
Content_zh: 因為這個里程碑會讓我們知道，如果能把 Cloud Colony 做到 Alpha、甚至 Beta，那就有意義。

Content: Not the end of all our problems, but it'll mean something.
Content_zh: 不代表所有問題都解決了，但會有意義。

Content: If we can't bring it to Beta, that'll be even more informative in a way.
Content_zh: 如果我們沒辦法把它做到 Beta，那某種程度上會更有參考價值。

Content: Schafer: But I never believe that we're going to be late, but I've been late before, a couple times.
Content_zh: Schafer：我從來不相信我們會延期，但我以前確實延期過幾次。

Content: Three or four. Every time, most of the times.
Content_zh: 三、四次吧。每次，幾乎每次。

Content: So we really want to prove that we can get the Cloud Colony done this month so we can prove that we're, we're picking up the pace
Content_zh: 所以我們真的想證明這個月能完成 Cloud Colony，證明我們正在加快速度，

Content: and we're, you know, we can finish stuff.
Content_zh: 而且我們，你知道，我們能把東西做完。

Content: Schafer: It's great. I mean this is what I've been waiting for, it's been really frustrating to be in the writing cave
Content_zh: Schafer：這很棒。這就是我一直在等的事，一直待在寫作洞裡真的很挫折，

Content: and the designing cave for a long time.
Content_zh: 還有設計洞裡，待了很久。

Content: 'Cause it's like work's being done on the game and I'm not participating in it.
Content_zh: 因為大家一直在做遊戲，但我沒有參與。

Content: and now I'm getting much more, being much more participatory.
Content_zh: 現在我更能實際參與了。

Content: And I feel like, I feel like I'm able to point at what needs to get done and push on it to get it done more.
Content_zh: 我覺得我能指出哪些事需要完成，然後推動它們完成。

Content: Schafer: There's two things like for me, now the first one was pretty much the same as this is, but it's just like harder to list out.
Content_zh: Schafer：對我來說有兩件事。第一件其實跟這個差不多，只是比較難列出來。

Content: Things that I'm looking to find answers for.
Content_zh: 也就是我想找到答案的事情。

Content: 'Cause I want find answers to almost everything, like is the game working?
Content_zh: 因為我幾乎什麼都想找答案，例如遊戲有沒有成立？

Content: Is the game right?
Content_zh: 這款遊戲方向對嗎？

Content: Is it, like you have this feeling after the sprint and like that's exactly what the game's going to look like,
Content_zh: 是不是像 sprint 結束後，你會有種感覺：對，遊戲大概就會長這樣，

Content: but as far, as close as we can except for the things that are obviously we can't.
Content_zh: 除了那些明顯做不到的部分，其他都盡量接近。

Content: That's the first one.
Content_zh: 那是第一件事。

Content: The second one sounds like, it sounds like a disagreement but it's not a disagreement.
Content_zh: 第二件聽起來像是意見不同，但其實不是。

Content: It's really just working at it from the other end.
Content_zh: 只是從另一個方向來看這件事。

Content: It's that I really want the Cloud Colony be done.
Content_zh: 就是我真的希望 Cloud Colony 能完成。

Content: I mean like done with the Cloud Colony, 'cause we've been working on it for long time
Content_zh: 我的意思是完成 Cloud Colony，因為我們已經做很久了，

Content: and we have a lot of other areas to get to.
Content_zh: 而且還有很多其他區域要做。

Content: And so I want to have it be, as much as possible, have the approach of like we're not coming back to the Cloud Colony.
Content_zh: 所以我希望盡可能用「我們不會再回頭做 Cloud Colony」的心態來處理。

Content: I want to be approaching it like we're finishing off in terms of Beta, you know.
Content_zh: 我想用接近 Beta 的收尾方式來看待它。

Content: Schafer: So we're going to go, actually go through the game play and make sure we itemize all the things that need to be done.
Content_zh: Schafer：所以我們會實際跑過遊戲流程，確認把所有需要完成的事項都列出來。

Content: Franzke: That's the idea.
Content_zh: Franzke：就是這個想法。

Content: All right.
Content_zh: 好。

Content: And you were, Greg is the official note taker.
Content_zh: 而 Greg 是正式的記錄員。

Content: Rice: Yes. Schafer: OK.
Content_zh: Rice：對。Schafer：好。

Content: So I'm trying to solve a puzzle.
Content_zh: 所以我正在解一個謎題。

Content: I'm trying to make Jessie like me.
Content_zh: 我想讓 Jessie 喜歡我。

Content: Jessie doesn't like me.
Content_zh: Jessie 不喜歡我。

Content: So when you go over here and you interact with Jessie, where the hell did I go?
Content_zh: 所以當你走到這裡跟 Jessie 互動時，我到底跑去哪了？

Content: There, what's that?
Content_zh: 那裡，那是什麼？

Content: What was that?
Content_zh: 剛剛那是什麼？

Content: Kipnis: That's if you click off the nav mesh or something like that.
Content_zh: Kipnis：那是你點到導航網格外面之類的地方。

Content: Like that, that happens.
Content_zh: 像那樣，就會發生。

Content: Schafer: Her turning animation's a little much.
Content_zh: Schafer：她轉身的動畫有點太長了。

Content: Kipnis: It's a little much? Do you want it to be shorter?
Content_zh: Kipnis：太長嗎？你想要短一點？

Content: Schafer: Well, it makes it really unresponsive that she has to do that turn.
Content_zh: Schafer：嗯，她得做那個轉身，會讓操作變得很不靈敏。

Content: You kind of want her to just move right away.
Content_zh: 你會希望她直接馬上移動。

Content: Rice: Play-throughs are awesome, yeah.
Content_zh: Rice：完整試玩很棒，對。

Content: 'Cause it gets everybody in the same room together looking at it at the same time.
Content_zh: 因為這能讓大家同時待在同一個房間裡一起看。

Content: It like nails Tim down to where like he has to like sit there and play it with everybody in a room and get directly like,
Content_zh: 這等於把 Tim 抓來，讓他得坐在房間裡跟大家一起玩，然後直接，

Content: point out the things that he's not happy with.
Content_zh: 指出他不滿意的地方。

Content: Because I mean, he plays it a lot and he'll send the email feedback and he'll bring things up in stand-ups and stuff.
Content_zh: 因為他其實常常玩，也會寄 email 回饋，還會在站會之類的場合提出來。

Content: But it's nice to just be able to sit there and like everybody see it on the screen together
Content_zh: 但能坐在那裡，讓大家一起在螢幕上看到，

Content: and like know the things that he is not happy with and that need work.
Content_zh: 知道他不滿意、需要處理的是哪些地方，這樣很好。

Content: Schafer: So first I have to solve that puzzle.
Content_zh: Schafer：所以我得先解開那個謎題。

Content: So I come back here and I'm like...
Content_zh: 所以我回到這裡，然後我就……

Content: [Groans] Schafer: So task for Oliver, make that crash optional.
Content_zh: [呻吟] Schafer：所以 Oliver 的任務是，把那個當機做成可選。

Content: Franzke: All right. Optional crash system.
Content_zh: Franzke：好。可選式當機系統。

Content: The game has encountered a problem.
Content_zh: 遊戲發生問題。

Content: Do you want to crash now?
Content_zh: 你現在要當機嗎？

Content: No. All right. Game is recovered.
Content_zh: 不要。好。遊戲已恢復。

Content: [Music] Schafer: One of the hard things about making an adventure game
Content_zh: [音樂] Schafer：做冒險遊戲很困難的一點，

Content: is that the team doesn't often find the game fun when they're working on it.
Content_zh: 是團隊在開發時通常不會覺得遊戲好玩。

Content: It's not like a first person shooter where even if you're working on a first person shooter, it's fun to play it.
Content_zh: 它不像第一人稱射擊遊戲，就算你正在做第一人稱射擊遊戲，玩起來還是好玩。

Content: But an adventure game, you know all the solutions, all the puzzles and you know all the jokes.
Content_zh: 但冒險遊戲，你知道所有解法、所有謎題，也知道所有笑點。

Content: So there's no like discovery or you know, you know, wonder or...
Content_zh: 所以就沒有什麼探索感，或是驚奇感，或……

Content: It's fun to see it come together and it's fun to see that you're making something good.
Content_zh: 看著它成形很有趣，看到自己正在做出好東西也很有趣。

Content: But you don't stay late.
Content_zh: 但你不會留下來加班。

Content: No one's going to stay late to like play Red's after, after hours.
Content_zh: 沒有人會下班後還留下來玩 Red’s。

Content: Hey, you want to play Red's?
Content_zh: 嘿，你想玩 Red’s 嗎？

Content: Yeah, let's do it.
Content_zh: 好啊，來玩。

Content: Let's hook it up.
Content_zh: 接起來吧。

Content: 'Cause like, oh, yeah, I know.
Content_zh: 因為你會想，喔，對，我知道。

Content: This is the puzzle I wired up.
Content_zh: 這是我接好的謎題。

Content: So and that's always been sad.
Content_zh: 所以這一直有點令人難過。

Content: Schafer: OK. I'm trying to remember how to actually make games.
Content_zh: Schafer：好。我正在努力想起來到底要怎麼做遊戲。

Content: I'm trying to program.
Content_zh: 我在試著寫程式。

Content: First let me get all the latest stuff.
Content_zh: 先讓我把最新的東西都抓下來。

Content: It's syncing up.
Content_zh: 正在同步。

Content: Now I'm going to run the game.
Content_zh: 現在我要跑遊戲。

Content: Never actually done this before.
Content_zh: 其實以前從沒做過這件事。

Content: This is gonna be embarrassing.
Content_zh: 這會很丟臉。

Content: We should film the second time I do this.
Content_zh: 我們應該拍我第二次做這件事。

Content: 'Cause the second time I'll be like [makes computer noises]
Content_zh: 因為第二次我就會像這樣：[模仿電腦聲]

Content: oh, this is Unix. I know this.
Content_zh: 喔，這是 Unix。我懂。

Content: [computer noises] Offscreen Voice: Is that Jurassic Park?
Content_zh: [電腦聲] 畫外音：那是《侏羅紀公園》嗎？

Content: Schafer: That Jurassic Park joke.
Content_zh: Schafer：就是那個《侏羅紀公園》的笑話。

Content: Is that getting too old to make that reference?
Content_zh: 現在還拿那個來講會不會太老梗？

Content: I'm hoping I can actually just enter dialogue myself into the engine.
Content_zh: 我希望我真的可以自己把對話輸入到引擎裡。

Content: 'Cause you know, when you, you know, for a long time from Psychonauts and Brutal Legend stuff,
Content_zh: 因為你知道，以前從《Psychonauts》和《Brütal Legend》那些案子開始，

Content: I'd write dialogue and then someone like Anna or Kee or someone would have to come and implement it.
Content_zh: 我會寫對話，然後 Anna、Kee 或其他人就得過來實作。

Content: But then you don't get to really like tweak it.
Content_zh: 但那樣你就沒辦法真的微調。

Content: Like I like to put stuff in and then see how the timing works out and then mess with it.
Content_zh: 我喜歡把東西放進去，看時間點效果如何，再去調整。

Content: And also it'd be nice while I was writing if I didn't think I was just creating a whole bunch of work for someone else to do.
Content_zh: 而且如果我寫作時不覺得自己只是在製造一大堆工作給別人做，也會很好。

Content: It'd be great if I could, if I'm going to put it into Excel or if I'm going to put it into a database,
Content_zh: 如果我反正要把它放進 Excel，或放進資料庫，

Content: I might as well just put it into the game.
Content_zh: 那不如直接放進遊戲裡。

Content: And then I'm not creating work for, you know, that way Anna can work on something else and I can just do this.
Content_zh: 這樣我就不會製造工作給別人，你知道，Anna 可以去做別的事，我自己做這個就好。

Content: And also at my age I really would like to learn something so I don't have Alzheimer's.
Content_zh: 而且以我這個年紀，我真的很想學點東西，免得得阿茲海默症。

Content: But I think it's too late.
Content_zh: 但我覺得太晚了。

Content: I think I have it 'cause I can't figure out at all what's happening.
Content_zh: 我覺得我已經有了，因為我完全搞不懂現在發生什麼事。

Content: How do I open this scene?
Content_zh: 我要怎麼打開這個場景？

Content: How do I open?
Content_zh: 我要怎麼開？

Content: Open in project?
Content_zh: 在專案中開啟？

Content: That's a bunch of things.
Content_zh: 那是一堆東西。

Content: Can you believe I have a CS degree?
Content_zh: 你能相信我有資工學位嗎？

Content: Class of '89.
Content_zh: 1989 年畢業班。

Content: Voice.
Content_zh: Voice。

Content: X vertical Font.
Content_zh: X vertical Font。

Content: Where's the dialogue?
Content_zh: 對話在哪裡？

Content: Where's your dialogue?
Content_zh: 你的對話在哪？

Content: I'm going to go ask her really quick.
Content_zh: 我馬上去問她。

Content: I'll be right back.
Content_zh: 我很快回來。

Content: Schafer: And then I'll totally be able to program this entire game myself.
Content_zh: Schafer：然後我就完全可以自己寫完整款遊戲了。

Content: I got it.
Content_zh: 我懂了。

Content: I totally had it all figured out.
Content_zh: 我完全都搞清楚了。

Content: Wait...
Content_zh: 等等……

Content: This looks totally different than on her computer.
Content_zh: 這跟她電腦上的看起來完全不一樣。

Content: Why did she set me up like this?
Content_zh: 她為什麼要這樣整我？

Content: She totally lied to me.
Content_zh: 她根本騙了我。

Content: I'm going to have to go back there and ask her again.
Content_zh: 我得回去再問她一次。

Content: Ugh.
Content_zh: 唉。

Content: Schafer: Like in theory, me being able to program my own dialogue in the game would save time.
Content_zh: Schafer：理論上，如果我能自己把對話實作進遊戲，應該會省時間。

Content: It's not there quite yet.
Content_zh: 但現在還沒到那個程度。

Content: See, I'm taking up Anna's time but it's going to save her time later, when I can program the entire game myself.
Content_zh: 你看，我現在佔用了 Anna 的時間，但等我能自己寫完整款遊戲時，就會幫她省時間。

Content: Let's see if I remember anything that Anna showed me.
Content_zh: 看看我還記不記得 Anna 教我的東西。

Content: I don't remember anything that Anna showed me.
Content_zh: 我完全不記得 Anna 教了我什麼。

Content: It's annoying because I did touch stuff and now I don't know if I touched something.
Content_zh: 很煩，因為我有動過東西，現在不知道自己到底碰了什麼。

Content: I pretty much reverted everything I touched.
Content_zh: 我差不多把碰過的東西都復原了。

Content: Schafer: So embarrassing.
Content_zh: Schafer：太丟臉了。

Content: Schafer: I got it. I'm in.
Content_zh: Schafer：我懂了。我進去了。

Content: I've hacked the mainframe.
Content_zh: 我駭進主機了。

Content: I'm totally, I'm seeing all the beauty that is Red's simultaneously at once.
Content_zh: 我一次看到了 Red’s 全部的美好。

Content: Well, that's a day's work. Done.
Content_zh: 好，這就是一天的工作。完成。

Content: Schafer: But there's this pressure to, we got to meet the milestone.
Content_zh: Schafer：但現在有壓力，我們得達成這個里程碑。

Content: I just got to get all this writing done.
Content_zh: 我得把這些文字全都寫完。

Content: I can't be messing around with like this stuff.
Content_zh: 我不能再搞這些東西。

Content: So I changed gears and just focus on getting all the dialogue done and it means that Anna has to spend a lot of time implementing it,
Content_zh: 所以我轉換方向，專心把所有對話寫完，這也代表 Anna 得花很多時間實作，

Content: but the question is will there be a point where I can actually, you know, get up and running enough on the programming to make that efficient?
Content_zh: 但問題是，會不會有一天我程式那邊真的能上手到足夠有效率？

Content: I don't know.
Content_zh: 我不知道。

Content: Kipnis: There's just a lot more content, I think, in these, in Cloud Colony than there has been in the other stuff that we've had.
Content_zh: Kipnis：我覺得 Cloud Colony 裡的內容，比我們之前做過的其他東西多很多。

Content: And it's a lot of work to get it all implemented.
Content_zh: 而且要把它全部實作出來是很大的工作量。

Content: Crook: I don't know. It'll be all right.
Content_zh: Crook：不知道。會沒事的。

Content: It'll be OK. It's always this way.
Content_zh: 會沒事的。一直都是這樣。

Content: Ryken: It's, I think back the first game that I worked on was Brutal Legend and that was, that was my concept for what, you know, games are like, you know.
Content_zh: Ryken：我回想自己參與的第一款遊戲是《Brütal Legend》，那時那就是我對遊戲開發的概念。

Content: And in this one it's like, it's not going to take four and a half years?
Content_zh: 而這次就像，這不會花四年半吧？

Content: Sounds like a good game for me.
Content_zh: 聽起來很適合我。

Content: But yeah, there's a lot of iteration in the beginning and then you find, you get closer to the spirit of the game
Content_zh: 不過對，一開始會有很多反覆修改，然後你會發現自己越來越接近遊戲的精神，

Content: and I feel like when the game starts gelling together, kind of like the way just recently the girl got a shadow.
Content_zh: 我覺得當遊戲開始成形，就像最近那個女孩有了影子。

Content: And I was like happier than high hell that Just the smallest little thing goes into the game and it,
Content_zh: 我當時超級開心，因為只要最小的一點東西放進遊戲裡，

Content: it's no longer just this, this animated stick figure walking around.
Content_zh: 它就不再只是走來走去的會動火柴人。

Content: It's gelling it together and it gets exciting, it gets exciting because like those things are going to be there as we're carving out
Content_zh: 它會讓整個東西開始成形，也會讓人興奮，因為那些東西會一直在那裡，當我們慢慢刻出

Content: those little connectors to the world that you're walking in, you know.
Content_zh: 你正在走的世界裡那些小連結時。

Content: They're going to be there when we start on new areas already and you're going to have a feel for walking through the spaces
Content_zh: 等我們開始做新區域時，它們就已經在那裡，你也會對穿過那些空間有感覺，

Content: and it's really cool.
Content_zh: 那真的很酷。

Content: Schafer: Look at that sinking!
Content_zh: Schafer：看她陷下去了！

Content: That's awesome.
Content_zh: 太棒了。

Content: I can't save her!
Content_zh: 我救不了她！

Content: I can't save her, I can't save her! Get up!
Content_zh: 我救不了她，我救不了她！起來！

Content: Oh my God, I've had this dream.
Content_zh: 天啊，我做過這個夢。

Content: Schafer: No, I get more excited about this game every day because as it starts to come together,
Content_zh: Schafer：不，我每天都對這款遊戲更興奮，因為它開始成形，

Content: because we started from just scratch, it's cool to actually see it become real, you know.
Content_zh: 因為我們是從零開始，所以真的看到它變成真實的東西很酷。

Content: Because of the framerate I can't click anywhere Schafer: But I think the game is looking a lot better,
Content_zh: 因為影格率的關係，我哪裡都點不了。Schafer：但我覺得遊戲看起來好多了，

Content: the characters are really pretty.
Content_zh: 角色真的很漂亮。

Content: The backgrounds are getting better and better looking.
Content_zh: 背景也越來越好看。

Content: And you know, we're starting to see the basic interface working now.
Content_zh: 而且你知道，我們開始看到基本介面能運作了。

Content: And with music in, you know, it's starting to really feel like a real adventure game.
Content_zh: 再加上音樂，你知道，它開始真的像一款真正的冒險遊戲。

Content: Ryken: And I was just doing the flat paint over on the art that we had that Lee did, sent out.
Content_zh: Ryken：我剛才是在 Lee 做好、傳出來的美術稿上做平塗。

Content: Schafer: All right, everybody.
Content_zh: Schafer：好，各位。

Content: Let us break.
Content_zh: 我們休息吧。

Content: And let us do this again next week.
Content_zh: 下週再來一次。

Content: Stapley: So yesterday I was just sort of going through and painting a bunch of the scenes files.
Content_zh: Stapley：昨天我大概就是一路處理，畫了一堆場景檔。

Content: And now I'm just addressing all that feedback from the walk-through, play-through.
Content_zh: 現在我在處理走查、完整試玩後收到的所有回饋。

Content: Ryken: Yesterday I was drawing hands for the girl and then I got sick of drawing hands for the girl.
Content_zh: Ryken：昨天我在畫女孩的手，後來畫到很煩。

Content: So I stopped drawing hands for the girl.
Content_zh: 所以我就不畫女孩的手了。

Content: Hands are the worst!
Content_zh: 手最討厭了！

Content: Why does anybody want to do hands?
Content_zh: 為什麼會有人想畫手？

Content: Petty: So now she has hooks?
Content_zh: Petty：所以她現在裝鉤子了？

Content: Ryken: Yeah. She's always going like this.
Content_zh: Ryken：對。她就一直這樣。

Content: Fietzek: And yesterday I did some last final changes on the forum post, which is online now.
Content_zh: Fietzek：昨天我對論壇貼文做了最後幾個修改，現在已經上線了。

Content: So also, this is my last stand-up, which is so sad.
Content_zh: 所以這也是我最後一次站會，真的好難過。

Content: And yeah. You know, like I, yeah, I want to say a tiny bit.
Content_zh: 然後，對。你知道，我，對，我想稍微說幾句。

Content: I'm not so good at this, but just a big thank you to all of you guys.
Content_zh: 我不太擅長這個，但真的非常謝謝大家。

Content: It's been an amazing three months.
Content_zh: 這三個月非常棒。

Content: You guys are all amazing and amazing people.
Content_zh: 你們都超棒，也都是很棒的人。

Content: I'm so thankful for that internship and I did a group picture of us.
Content_zh: 我很感謝這次實習，也畫了一張我們的團體圖。

Content: Kipnis: Awww!
Content_zh: Kipnis：喔！

Content: Franzke: That's awesome.
Content_zh: Franzke：太棒了。

Content: [Laughter] Everyone on the team, including you guys.
Content_zh: [笑聲] 團隊裡的每個人，包括你們。

Content: Kipnis: Aww dude, thanks a lot. Bagel: Lets just scan that in.
Content_zh: Kipnis：哇，老兄，太謝謝了。Bagel：我們直接把它掃描進去吧。

Content: Fietzek: I'll send it around.
Content_zh: Fietzek：我會寄給大家。

Content: Kipnis: Should we make it poster size?
Content_zh: Kipnis：要不要做成海報尺寸？

Content: Fietzek: It should be big enough.
Content_zh: Fietzek：應該夠大了。

Content: Petty: Thank you, Marius.
Content_zh: Petty：謝謝你，Marius。

Content: Thank you for all your hard work here.
Content_zh: 謝謝你在這裡付出的所有努力。

Content: It's been awesome having you here.
Content_zh: 有你在真的很棒。

Content: [Applause] Schafer: I thought we might be able to convince Marius to stay
Content_zh: [掌聲] Schafer：我本來以為我們也許能說服 Marius 留下來，

Content: because we would have had to just derail his education as I said, but there was, I was not expecting the real reason
Content_zh: 因為如我所說，我們大概只需要毀掉他的學業，但我沒想到真正的理由，

Content: he was going back to Germany, which was for love.
Content_zh: 他回德國是為了愛。

Content: And though we are evil enough to end education, we could not fight the force that is love.
Content_zh: 雖然我們夠邪惡，可以終結教育，但我們對抗不了愛的力量。

Content: Schafer: We were trying to set up a contract where he could keep doing what he's doing from Germany, making animatics for us.
Content_zh: Schafer：我們試著安排一份合約，讓他能從德國繼續做現在做的事，替我們做動態分鏡。

Content: It's kind of like when Scott Campbell and Nathan moved to New York.
Content_zh: 有點像 Scott Campbell 和 Nathan 搬去紐約的時候。

Content: I would just try and keep those guys working for us because I, you don't, you know, people, people who are talented people
Content_zh: 我就是會試著讓那些人繼續替我們工作，因為你知道，有才華的人，

Content: don't come around every day and you got to like hang onto them when you can.
Content_zh: 不是每天都能遇到；可以的時候就得抓住他們。

Content: Marius: Yeah. I can try coming back next year.
Content_zh: Marius：對。我可以試著明年再回來。

Content: [Music] Schafer: I feel like now we're actually moving at the speed that we're moving at.
Content_zh: [音樂] Schafer：我覺得現在我們終於真的知道自己的速度了。

Content: As soon as they get all locked down with the process between Bagel and Levi and Lee,
Content_zh: 等 Bagel、Levi 和 Lee 之間的流程都固定下來，

Content: I think it'll be, feel like we're moving at a good clip.
Content_zh: 我覺得就會感覺我們正在以不錯的速度前進。

Content: Offscreen Voice: So you're back?
Content_zh: 畫外音：所以你回來了？

Content: Stapley: I'm back in action. Red's.
Content_zh: Stapley：我回來開工了。Red’s。

Content: No way man.
Content_zh: 不可能，老兄。

Content: Tim should wear my hat.
Content_zh: Tim 應該戴我的帽子。

Content: [Music] Stapley: I'd like to actually get involved more with like concept stuff, though.
Content_zh: [音樂] Stapley：不過我其實想更多參與概念設計那類工作。

Content: Yeah, I feel like I'm just kind of a painter.
Content_zh: 對，我覺得自己比較像只是負責畫的人。

Content: Petty: Well, you and I talked about that, right?
Content_zh: Petty：嗯，你跟我聊過這件事，對吧？

Content: And you said you, Stapley: Yeah, yeah, but like...
Content_zh: 然後你說你，Stapley：對，對，可是……

Content: Petty: you wanted to do, Stapley: the way it's turning with, because all the designs are approved, so it's basically like, it's approved.
Content_zh: Petty：你想做，Stapley：現在的走向是，因為所有設計都核准了，所以基本上就是已經定案。

Content: You know what I mean?
Content_zh: 你懂我的意思嗎？

Content: Petty: Individuals have changed what they wanted to do.
Content_zh: Petty：每個人想做的事情都變了。

Content: You know, initially Bagel did want to do a lot of painting.
Content_zh: 你知道，一開始 Bagel 確實想做很多繪製工作。

Content: And I think as we started getting into that, he felt like hey, you know, I really want to be more on the compositional side, which is fine, you know.
Content_zh: 我想當我們開始進入那個階段，他覺得，嘿，你知道，我其實更想做構圖那一端，這也沒問題。

Content: But that means we're going to have to shift things around.
Content_zh: 但那代表我們得把工作重新調整一下。

Content: Petty: Do you think this process is going to make that better?
Content_zh: Petty：你覺得這個流程會讓情況變好嗎？

Content: Stapley: Which process?
Content_zh: Stapley：哪個流程？

Content: Petty: Whatever, we're sort of being non-specific...
Content_zh: Petty：隨便啦，我們講得有點不具體……

Content: Stapley: I think having more hands painting and oh, well, I don't know.
Content_zh: Stapley：我覺得如果有更多人一起畫，然後，呃，我不知道。

Content: I think I would feel, well, at least if we're going to say like this is Bagel's style of game,
Content_zh: 我覺得至少如果我們要說這是 Bagel 風格的遊戲，

Content: then I would, yeah, at least I would have a little bit more like yeah, you know, that is my stuff.
Content_zh: 那我至少會比較有一點，對，你知道，那裡面也有我的東西。

Content: Stapley: Like this sacrifice scene.
Content_zh: Stapley：像這個獻祭場景。

Content: This is actually, I did the layout in this one and the camera angles.
Content_zh: 這個其實是我做了構圖和鏡頭角度。

Content: And I feel good how that went.
Content_zh: 我覺得那部分效果不錯。

Content: Offscreen Voice: It's more Bagel.
Content_zh: 畫外音：這比較像 Bagel。

Content: Stapley: Yeah, I feel, yeah, like this is maybe more Bagel.
Content_zh: Stapley：對，我覺得，對，這可能比較像 Bagel。

Content: A little bit.
Content_zh: 有一點。

Content: Petty: Click down, like just double click towards the bottom so she walks down there.
Content_zh: Petty：往下點，像是朝下方雙擊，讓她走到那裡。

Content: Schafer: What the? Whoa..!
Content_zh: Schafer：什麼？哇！

Content: Schafer: Yeah, I like the, the first tested out was the sacrifice area, the new method.
Content_zh: Schafer：對，我喜歡的是，第一個拿新方法測試的是獻祭區域。

Content: And I really liked that.
Content_zh: 我真的很喜歡。

Content: It's pretty and I like that whole setup.
Content_zh: 它很漂亮，我喜歡整個配置。

Content: I like the crazy big bird cages that are moved and ripped open and I think those are cool.
Content_zh: 我喜歡那些被移開、撕開的超大鳥籠，我覺得很酷。

Content: So it's promising.
Content_zh: 所以很有潛力。

Content: Stapley: Yeah. So now it's just, I guess there's just a question of like talking to Levi and Lee this week
Content_zh: Stapley：對。所以現在我想就只剩下這週要跟 Levi 和 Lee 談，

Content: and figuring out the paint, how to make it look consistent, you know.
Content_zh: 弄清楚畫法，要怎麼讓它看起來一致。

Content: That's, I think, the big major thing right now.
Content_zh: 我覺得那是現在最主要的大事。

Content: All right, you know, that's the thing, a worrisome thing that we should work on.
Content_zh: 好，你知道，這就是我們該處理的一個令人擔心的點。

Content: Petty: So this is the very old one, right?
Content_zh: Petty：所以這是很舊的版本，對吧？

Content: Based on the color key.
Content_zh: 以 color key 為基礎。

Content: So, I painted this one entirely with a charcoal brush, 'cause when we started I thought that's what we're doing on that one.
Content_zh: 我整張都是用炭筆筆刷畫的，因為一開始我以為那張就是要那樣做。

Content: So it's, I think it's, it's a little too linear.
Content_zh: 所以我覺得它有點太線條化。

Content: It's too charcoal-y and colors are all wrong.
Content_zh: 太像炭筆，顏色也全都不對。

Content: Petty: It's been difficult.
Content_zh: Petty：這一直很困難。

Content: It's weird, it's weird finding out which area, why it's been difficult.
Content_zh: 很奇怪，要弄清楚到底是哪個區域、為什麼難，這件事本身也很怪。

Content: Part of it has been has been, he's evolving his own approach, to like the clouds specifically is very different than
Content_zh: 其中一部分是，他自己的做法也在演變，尤其是雲的處理方式，跟

Content: sort of what painted the lumberjack.
Content_zh: 我們畫伐木工那邊時用的方式很不一樣。

Content: So as his approach has been evolving, I've been trying to like paint this way and we've been changing it back and forth.
Content_zh: 所以隨著他的做法改變，我一直試著用這種方式畫，我們也一直來回調整。

Content: Petty: I kind of feel I knew the way we should try handling this one is, if you, say take this scene
Content_zh: Petty：我有點覺得，我知道這個場景應該怎麼試著處理；如果你拿這個場景，

Content: and do like a line drawing composition and then do like a little small color key.
Content_zh: 先做線稿構圖，再做一個小小的 color key。

Content: And then we give it to Levi and have him try and paint it.
Content_zh: 然後我們交給 Levi，讓他試著畫。

Content: Ryken: I feel like we wouldn't need that much of a color key, considering we were coming from an establishing shot already.
Content_zh: Ryken：我覺得我們不需要那麼多 color key，畢竟我們已經有前面的定場鏡頭了。

Content: Petty: We've been doing other things as well, as like, you know, I have brush sets that I made basing on he likes to use in Photoshop
Content_zh: Petty：我們也一直在做其他事，像是你知道，我有做一些筆刷組，是根據他在 Photoshop 裡喜歡用的東西做的，

Content: and we're kind of updating those.
Content_zh: 然後我們也在更新那些。

Content: So those can be given to all the artists that are painting digitally.
Content_zh: 這樣就能交給所有做數位繪製的美術。

Content: It's difficult.
Content_zh: 這很困難。

Content: I mean, I think one of the reasons why it made sense to have him paint stuff on the back end is it was,
Content_zh: 我的意思是，我覺得讓他在後段處理繪製有道理的原因之一是，

Content: it would have been, he'd be the last person touching each asset.
Content_zh: 他會是每個素材最後經手的人。

Content: So it's necessarily just going to be more unified stylistically.
Content_zh: 所以風格上自然會比較統一。

Content: But by moving him to the front, it creates more, it probably gives the overall sense of things a little more quirkiness that he would have.
Content_zh: 但把他移到前段後，可能會讓整體感覺多一些他的怪趣味。

Content: But it means we have more potential like consistency drifting going on, on the backside.
Content_zh: 但這也代表後段比較可能出現風格一致性跑掉的問題。

Content: But yeah, I think it's been, everything's been moving pretty well actually.
Content_zh: 不過對，我覺得其實一切進展得滿順利。

Content: I'd say we made lots of really good progress this sprint.
Content_zh: 我會說這次 sprint 我們取得了很大的進展。

Content: Dillon: Like I'm actually really happy with the progress that we made in terms of this stuff.
Content_zh: Dillon：我其實真的很滿意我們在這些事情上的進度。

Content: We got a bunch of big features in and the tool's a lot better, kind of stuff.
Content_zh: 我們加進了一堆大型功能，工具也好用多了。

Content: Rice: Just the little things, too, just like the cameras feel so much better.
Content_zh: Rice：還有一些小地方也是，像鏡頭系統感覺好多了。

Content: Just navigating around is way better now.
Content_zh: 現在到處移動順暢多了。

Content: Dillon: It's just one of those things where it's like, you know, getting a basic system in there is easy and
Content_zh: Dillon：這就是那種，你知道，把基本系統做進去很容易，

Content: giving it enough love so that it actually feels good is hard.
Content_zh: 但要花足夠心力讓它真的用起來舒服，這很難。

Content: Like having milestones like this where we're really focused on polishing up an area,
Content_zh: 像這樣有里程碑，讓我們真正專注在打磨某個區域，

Content: is a forcing function for like we have a camera system but what we actually need is a good camera system.
Content_zh: 會逼我們面對：我們有鏡頭系統，但真正需要的是好的鏡頭系統。

Content: [Laughter] Rice: Yeah.
Content_zh: [笑聲] Rice：對。

Content: Dillon: So I think we may have gotten a lot of traction in a lot of different areas and that stuff this time around.
Content_zh: Dillon：所以我覺得這次我們在很多不同方面都有不少進展。

Content: Rice: Cool. Thanks, bro.
Content_zh: Rice：酷。謝啦，兄弟。

Content: Rice: Oh, what? How successful is the Cloud Colony stuff gonna be?
Content_zh: Rice：喔，什麼？Cloud Colony 這些東西到底會完成到什麼程度？

Content: Yeah, how close are you going to be to it actually being finished .
Content_zh: 對，離真正完成會有多近？

Content: Rice: Well, I think we'll have a better idea of that after I go around and talk to everybody, which I'm going to do right now.
Content_zh: Rice：嗯，我想等我去找每個人聊完之後，會比較清楚，我現在就要去做。

Content: Crook: But it's nice.
Content_zh: Crook：但這很好。

Content: This is working, which is really great to have.
Content_zh: 這已經能運作，真的很棒。

Content: So you know, Rice: Yeah.
Content_zh: 所以你知道，Rice：對。

Content: Three weeks ago we didn't have any cut scenes.
Content_zh: 三週前我們還沒有任何過場動畫。

Content: Crook: It's been sort of stuttering and going, you know, but that's smoothing out so we're making progress.
Content_zh: Crook：之前有點走走停停，你知道，但現在順起來了，所以我們有進展。

Content: Just not as fast as we would like.
Content_zh: 只是沒有我們希望的那麼快。

Content: Johnstone: So he's cleaned up. Rice: She.
Content_zh: Johnstone：所以他清完了。Rice：是她。

Content: Johnstone: She, she. She's cleaned up.
Content_zh: Johnstone：她，她。她清完了。

Content: And I'm really done in like five minutes.
Content_zh: 我真的大概五分鐘後就完成。

Content: Rice: Sweet. Johnstone: Jessie's.
Content_zh: Rice：太好了。Johnstone：Jessie 的。

Content: Rice: Awesome. Cool.
Content_zh: Rice：太棒了。酷。

Content: Johnstone: So that's where I'm in.
Content_zh: Johnstone：所以我現在進度就是這樣。

Content: That's where I'm at.
Content_zh: 這就是我目前的狀態。

Content: Rice: Perfect.
Content_zh: Rice：完美。

Content: Why is raining on your screen?
Content_zh: 為什麼你的螢幕在下雨？

Content: Johnstone: Oh. It's rainy moods as a soundtrack.
Content_zh: Johnstone：喔。那是 Rainy Mood 當背景音。

Content: It's nice.
Content_zh: 很不錯。

Content: Sometimes it's nice to listen to ambient noise.
Content_zh: 有時候聽環境音滿舒服的。

Content: Rice: Cool.
Content_zh: Rice：酷。

Content: Johnstone: So why not?
Content_zh: Johnstone：所以為什麼不呢？

Content: Rice: This isn't going as fast as I was hoping it would.
Content_zh: Rice：這進度沒有我希望的那麼快。

Content: Miss Kipnis, I'm coming to you.
Content_zh: Kipnis 小姐，我來找你了。

Content: Kipnis: So I mean, I don't, there's no way I'm going to get through my entire list, but I'll get, you know, I'll get as much as I can.
Content_zh: Kipnis：我是說，我不可能把整張清單都做完，但我會盡量做。

Content: Rice: Cool. Cool.
Content_zh: Rice：酷，酷。

Content: Petty: You know, there's not a ton left.
Content_zh: Petty：你知道，其實剩下的沒有很多。

Content: I mean, I'm pretty much on schedule.
Content_zh: 我是說，我大致上有照進度。

Content: I'm running a little behind but it's only theoretically behind.
Content_zh: 我有點落後，但只是理論上落後。

Content: Rice: Sounds good, man. Petty: Yeah.
Content_zh: Rice：聽起來不錯，老兄。Petty：對。

Content: Rice: We'll see how the play-through goes tomorrow.
Content_zh: Rice：明天看看完整試玩會怎麼樣。

Content: Schafer: Before we start, I'm going to guess that, like our goal was to finish Cloud Colony this sprint.
Content_zh: Schafer：開始之前，我先猜一下，我們原本的目標是這個 sprint 完成 Cloud Colony。

Content: I have a feeling it's not finished.
Content_zh: 我有種感覺它還沒完成。

Content: Rice: You might be right.
Content_zh: Rice：你可能是對的。

Content: Schafer: Oh man.
Content_zh: Schafer：天啊。

Content: I haven't even, we have like another two week sprint that it would be great if we could finish Cloud Colony in that.
Content_zh: 我甚至，我們還有另一個兩週 sprint；如果能在那段時間完成 Cloud Colony 就太好了。

Content: I don't want to just keep setting up the team to fail, though.
Content_zh: 不過我不想一直讓團隊陷入注定失敗的狀況。

Content: Like OK you guys, we're going to finish Cloud Colony this sprint.
Content_zh: 像是，好，各位，這個 sprint 我們要完成 Cloud Colony。

Content: OK, I didn't do it.
Content_zh: 好，我沒做到。

Content: OK, this time we're definitely going to finish. OK, no.
Content_zh: 好，這次我們一定會完成。好，還是沒有。

Content: That didn't work either, but OK, this sprint is the one.
Content_zh: 那也沒成功，但好，這次 sprint 一定行。

Content: So I don't want to say that, but there still is a lot to do.
Content_zh: 所以我不想那樣說，但確實還有很多事要做。

Content: I mean, you can't right now play the three puzzle branches, any of them to the end.
Content_zh: 我是說，你現在還沒辦法把三條謎題分支的任何一條玩到結尾。

Content: Rice: So yeah. Try to figure out now what you'll be looking at.
Content_zh: Rice：所以對，現在先試著弄清楚你要看的會是哪些東西。

Content: Schafer: Yeah.
Content_zh: Schafer：對。

Content: So we don't know what's going to happen because, even if this sprint did finish, if we'd finish Cloud Colony we'd be looking at like,
Content_zh: 所以我們不知道會怎樣，因為即使這個 sprint 真的完成 Cloud Colony，接下來也會是，

Content: looking at the design of the game and how much time we have.
Content_zh: 要看遊戲設計，以及我們還剩多少時間。

Content: That we would not probably finish the game in April as we had previously thought.
Content_zh: 也就是我們大概不會像之前想的那樣在四月完成遊戲。

Content: Rice: Um hm. Schafer: Which we're still thinking we're going to finish in April, Rice: No. Schafer: we've done some math that means, Rice: We did some equations.
Content_zh: Rice：嗯哼。Schafer：雖然我們還在想四月完成，Rice：不。Schafer：我們算過，那代表，Rice：我們列了方程式。

Content: Schafer: And if we go past April that would mean going past our money that we got from Kickstarter, which would be not great to do.
Content_zh: Schafer：如果超過四月，就代表會超出 Kickstarter 募到的資金，這樣不太好。

Content: We're having discussions with Isa and Justin about what, how we want to deal with that.
Content_zh: 我們正在跟 Isa 和 Justin 討論要怎麼處理。

Content: So I don't think we're going to conclude any of that in this meeting, but that's the problem that we're working on outside of this, too.
Content_zh: 所以我不覺得我們會在這場會議裡得出結論，但這也是我們在會議外處理的問題。

Content: Schafer: So see how much we did get done.
Content_zh: Schafer：所以來看看我們到底完成了多少。

Content: Rice: Yes. So there's, I have two files on Google Drive.
Content_zh: Rice：對。我在 Google Drive 上有兩個檔案。

Content: One is the budget, one is schedule.
Content_zh: 一個是預算，一個是時程。

Content: The original schedule that we made was like an attempt to get as many people on the team as we can
Content_zh: 我們最早做的時程，是試著讓團隊裡盡可能多人都排進去，

Content: and try to include like all of the things outside of that camp that we need to pay for.
Content_zh: 也試著把團隊以外那些需要花錢的事項都納入。

Content: And then like fit a game into that time, essentially right.
Content_zh: 然後基本上把一款遊戲塞進那段時間裡，對吧。

Content: And like as we were progressing like it was obvious that we weren't tracking to anything that was going to fit in that time.
Content_zh: 但隨著進度推進，很明顯照那個進度，做不出能塞進那段時間的遊戲。

Content: So now the hope is to try to start from scratch on a new schedule, taking in account like what we've seen with Cloud Colony
Content_zh: 所以現在希望從零開始做一份新時程，把 Cloud Colony 這次讓我們看到的狀況，

Content: and how long it's taken us to do that.
Content_zh: 以及我們花了多久做到這些，都考慮進去。

Content: How many other additional screens and areas are left in the game as its currently spec'd
Content_zh: 遊戲依目前規格還剩下多少額外畫面和區域，

Content: and try to take a new stab based on what we know now, at how long we think it'll actually take to make the game that you are designing.
Content_zh: 再根據目前知道的情況重新估一次，看你正在設計的那款遊戲實際會花多久。

Content: Stamos: So the new budget for this scenario includes going all the way to September and the increased team size,
Content_zh: Stamos：所以這個情境的新預算包含一路做到九月，以及增加團隊規模，

Content: which the increased team size is, you know, like four or five people.
Content_zh: 而增加的團隊規模，你知道，大概是四、五個人。

Content: Bailey: And has like 1.1 million dollars more costs.
Content_zh: Bailey：而且成本大概多了一百一十萬美元。

Content: Stamos: Yes. But the thing about this schedule is that it assumes at a certain point that systems work gets done
Content_zh: Stamos：對。但這份時程的重點是，它假設到某個時間點系統工作會完成，

Content: and that the team gains efficiency and the number of scenes almost doubles at a certain point in the project in order to get it done.
Content_zh: 而團隊效率會提高，到了專案某個階段，能完成的場景數幾乎會翻倍，才能把它完成。

Content: So it's actually pretty aggressive.
Content_zh: 所以其實相當激進。

Content: Bailey: Then I have to go find, I need to find money.
Content_zh: Bailey：那我就得去找錢了，我得找到錢。

Content: Stamos: Yeah. That is the concern.
Content_zh: Stamos：對。這就是我們擔心的點。

Content: Bailey: OK. Because right now, you know, it looks like, you know, the plane's kind of diving down a little bit
Content_zh: Bailey：好。因為現在看起來，飛機有點在往下俯衝，

Content: and we just need to, we have time to pull up.
Content_zh: 但我們還有時間把它拉起來。

Content: Schafer: What does the plane diving down stand for?
Content_zh: Schafer：飛機往下俯衝代表什麼？

Content: Bailey: That's, just Schafer: Us crashing? Bailey: Yeah.
Content_zh: Bailey：那就是，Schafer：我們墜機？Bailey：對。

Content: Schafer: The whole company crashing into the ground.
Content_zh: Schafer：整間公司撞到地上。

Content: Is that what you're saying or are you being, Bailey: I mean, the nicest, so the thing is that Red's, this project is,
Content_zh: 你是這個意思嗎？還是你在，Bailey：我是說，用最委婉的說法，重點是 Red’s 這個專案，

Content: it's going to determine the studio's success going forward.
Content_zh: 會決定工作室未來能不能成功。

Content: It's a major portion of my forecast of the health of the studio.
Content_zh: 它是我評估工作室未來營運狀況時非常重要的一部分。

Content: It hitting next year is absolutely necessary and how successful it is once it, you know, is out there and it's live, you know,
Content_zh: 它明年一定要推出，而且推出後、正式上線後有多成功，

Content: determines our ability to be independent and kind of have our own creative control, like that we're enjoying now.
Content_zh: 會決定我們能不能保持獨立，並擁有像現在這樣的創作自主權。

Content: So...
Content_zh: 所以……

Content: Schafer: It just seems wrong to make it cost more than we got.
Content_zh: Schafer：讓它花得比我們拿到的錢還多，感覺就是不對。

Content: Because we were so lucky to get the money that we got, because I'm going to feel like this moral thing to finish it
Content_zh: 因為我們很幸運才拿到那筆錢，所以我會覺得有種道德責任，要用那筆錢，

Content: for that amount of money or less.
Content_zh: 或更少的錢，把它完成。

Content: And I'm sure our business guy would love for us to finish for less.
Content_zh: 而且我相信我們的商務人員會很希望我們用更少的錢完成。

Content: But the game also has to be really good.
Content_zh: 但遊戲也必須真的很棒。

Content: And it is an important game to the studio.
Content_zh: 而且這對工作室來說是一款重要的遊戲。

Content: And strategically it's an important game for us because we own it.
Content_zh: 策略上它也對我們很重要，因為我們擁有它。

Content: We will, we don't have to share the money with any sort of other party, investor or publisher, you know, it's all ours.
Content_zh: 我們不用把收入分給任何其他方、投資人或發行商，你知道，收入全都是我們的。

Content: So it makes sense for us to make a really good game.
Content_zh: 所以我們很該做出一款真正好的遊戲。

Content: Bailey: We have to, we have to deliver on the vision and I don't think it's going to be done in the budget.
Content_zh: Bailey：我們必須實現這個願景，而我不覺得它能在預算內完成。

Content: So we have to find more money.
Content_zh: 所以我們得找到更多錢。

Content: And I feel like not only does it need to deliver on what the community is hoping it is
Content_zh: 而且我覺得它不只需要達到社群的期待，

Content: and the people that actually backed it, but the whole community in general and the crowd funding that's actually occurred since then, we're the poster child.
Content_zh: 和真正贊助它的人的期待，也牽動整個社群，以及之後出現的群眾募資；我們是代表案例。

Content: And we're the poster child, you know, succeed or fail.
Content_zh: 而我們就是代表案例，你知道，不管成功或失敗。

Content: And so, you know, we'd like to see more games funded this way.
Content_zh: 所以，你知道，我們希望看到更多遊戲用這種方式募資。

Content: Rice: I think it's also just important to keep in mind that there's like other impacts besides just funding the game.
Content_zh: Rice：我覺得也要記得，除了替遊戲找資金以外，還有其他影響。

Content: And that like if we now go get a publisher for this game then it's suddenly like voids everything we've said about the project
Content_zh: 像是如果我們現在去替這款遊戲找發行商，那突然就等於推翻了我們對這個專案說過的一切，

Content: and everything we're trying to do.
Content_zh: 以及我們一直想做的一切。

Content: It's hard and I know like we're looking at a number that's large, that we're saying we need.
Content_zh: 這很難，我也知道我們現在看著一個很大的數字，說我們需要這筆錢。

Content: But like as much as possible just trying to make sure that it's as good of money as this money was.
Content_zh: 但要盡量確保那筆錢跟這筆錢一樣沒有附帶條件。

Content: Bailey: I think, you know, there are, obviously there are financial realities that we have to be conscious of, but I mean the reality is that, you know,
Content_zh: Bailey：我想，你知道，顯然有些財務現實是我們必須正視的，但現實是，

Content: it needs to be a Double Fine game and the quality and it has to deliver on Tim's vision
Content_zh: 它需要是一款 Double Fine 遊戲，品質要到位，也必須實現 Tim 的願景。

Content: and so it comes back to, you know, can we, what would be like the minimum scope of the game
Content_zh: 所以問題又回到，你知道，我們能不能，遊戲的最小範圍會是什麼樣子，

Content: that you would feel it's still, delivers on how you envisioned it.
Content_zh: 才會讓你覺得它仍然有達到你想像中的樣子。

Content: Schafer: I mean, every time I've been up against this kind of situation, which is almost every time, there's scope
Content_zh: Schafer：我的意思是，每次我遇到這種情況，幾乎每次都會牽涉到範圍，

Content: and then there's money, then there's efficiencies and I've never seen this solved any other way than all of the above.
Content_zh: 然後是錢，還有效率；而我從沒看過除了全部都做之外的解法。

Content: Stamos: Yeah. Schafer: You know what I mean?
Content_zh: Stamos：對。Schafer：你懂我的意思嗎？

Content: Some scope reduction, some efficiency improvements, some extra money.
Content_zh: 縮小一些範圍、提高一些效率、增加一些資金。

Content: Not like any one of those things being the answer.
Content_zh: 不是其中任何一項單獨就是答案。

Content: Bailey: Ok. Ok.
Content_zh: Bailey：好，好。

Content: Rice: We can get it done.
Content_zh: Rice：我們可以完成的。

Content: Bailey: Should we do like an all hands in and team?
Content_zh: Bailey：我們要不要來個大家把手疊上去的團隊打氣？

Content: [Laughter] Rice: Yeah man.
Content_zh: [笑聲] Rice：對啊，老兄。

Content: Rice: OK.
Content_zh: Rice：好。

Content: Bailey: Alright guys.
Content_zh: Bailey：好，各位。

Content: Rice: OK. Let's keep those.
Content_zh: Rice：好。那些先留著。

Content: Rice: Ultimately the number one decider in the schedule is when do we feel like we have a game that we're proud of.
Content_zh: Rice：到頭來，時程裡最重要的決定因素，是我們什麼時候覺得自己有一款值得驕傲的遊戲。

Content: So it's trying to figure out the best plan of attack to get us to where we feel proud of the game and are ready to put it out.
Content_zh: 所以就是要找出最好的作戰計畫，讓我們做到對遊戲感到驕傲，並準備好推出。

Content: But I think we're safe to assume at this point that it's definitely past April.
Content_zh: 但我覺得現在應該可以先認定，它肯定會超過四月。

Content: So we just gotta kind of figure out when that is.
Content_zh: 所以我們只要弄清楚會延到什麼時候。

Content: And how we're going to get there.
Content_zh: 以及我們要怎麼走到那裡。

Content: We'll handle it.
Content_zh: 我們會處理好的。

Content: [Music] Schafer: What we've really done
Content_zh: [音樂] Schafer：我們真正做的事，

Content: is we've really banked the entire company on this one project and if even one thing goes wrong, it'll take down the whole games industry.
Content_zh: 是把整間公司都押在這一個專案上，而且只要有一件事出錯，就會拖垮整個遊戲產業。

Content: Is that kind of what you're, or you mean it like more like a Phil Fish thing?
Content_zh: 你是這個意思嗎？還是你是說比較像 Phil Fish 那種事？

Content: Now that, you're gonna use that.
Content_zh: 這段你一定會用。

Content: That's when you know.
Content_zh: 這種時候就知道了。

Content: Listen, that was copyright Phil Fish.
Content_zh: 聽著，那段是 Phil Fish 版權所有。

Content: I can't do that.
Content_zh: 我不能那樣做。
